breaking.title: 1 Dead, 10 Injured In Texas Shooting, Suspect Fired At Cop Days Earlier
breaking.summary: Police have provided limited details about how the shooting unfolded

breaking.title: Trump says U.S. military strike killed leader of Tren de Aragua gang with help from Venezuela
breaking.summary: Tren de Aragua has been labelled by the United States as a terrorist organisation; Guerrero Flores was charged in a New York federal court with racketeering conspiracy and other crimes, including lending support to terrorists

breaking.title: Jaishankar speaks to Rubio, lodges strong protest over U.S. Navy attacks that killed three Indians
breaking.summary: “Spoke to U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio this evening. I reiterated India’s strong protest at the attacks by the U.S. Navy in the Gulf that killed three Indian mariners,” Mr. Jaishankar said.

breaking.title: West Asia War updates: Abbas Araghchi says draft U.S. deal includes ending naval blockade, Hormuz arrangements
breaking.summary: Iran Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Israel looking to derail potential deal with U.S.

breaking.title: 'Driven to London by masked men': Indian-origin man Gagandeep Singh sentenced to 34-years for kidnaping, raping woman
breaking.summary: An Indian-origin man, Gagandeep Singh, has been sentenced to 34 years in prison in the UK for kidnapping, torturing, and raping a woman. The 34-year-old was convicted of multiple serious offenses after a trial. He will serve 28 years in custody, followed by an extended license period, and will be deported after completing his sentence.

breaking.title: The human cost of a deadly weed killer: banning of  paraqua in Telangana
breaking.summary: In March, Telangana banned paraquat, one of the world’s most widely used and highly toxic weedicides, becoming only the third State in India to do so. The herbicide is responsible for hundreds of deaths each year, most of them by taking their own lives, and some accidental. Serish Nanisetti reports on the campaign that led to the ban and why much more remains to be done

breaking.title: Peace Deal Soon? US Official 80-85% Confident, But Iran Has Some Conditions
breaking.summary: Though pitched as a major diplomatic victory, the proposed agreement will only establish a 60-day ceasefire that would be followed by another set of negotiations.

breaking.title: 1,569 days and counting: Russia-Ukraine war now longer than World War I
breaking.summary: The Russia-Ukraine war has surpassed World War I in duration, now exceeding 1,569 days. What began as a swift Russian operation has become Europe's longest and bloodiest conflict since WWII, defying early predictions. Despite diplomatic efforts and Western support for Ukraine, a durable peace remains elusive, with no clear end in sight.
